controls.name: HTTPS-only endpoints
controls.evidence: ACM certificate via ALB module; ssl-redirect configured in testnet (alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: 443) and production ingress (nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: true, force-ssl-redirect: true); TLS 1.2+ referenced in staging ingress. Live TLS 1.3 verification blocked by network environment.
controls.raw_output: infra/kubernetes/overlays/testnet/alb-ingress.yaml: alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: 443; infra/kubernetes/overlays/production/alb-ingress.yaml: alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: 443; infra/kubernetes/overlays/production/ingress.yaml: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: true, force-ssl-redirect: true

controls.name: API authentication
controls.evidence: auth.mjs exports authenticateHeaders; server.mjs imports and applies auth on every route, returning 401/403 on failure with structured audit log.
controls.raw_output: tools/compliance-gateway/src/auth.mjs: authenticateHeaders; tools/compliance-gateway/src/server.mjs: const auth = authenticateHeaders(req.headers, authState, requiredPermission); if (!auth.ok) { sendJson(res, auth.status, { error: auth.error }, req); }

controls.name: Rate limiting
controls.evidence: WAF module defines rate_limit variable (2000 req/5min per IP) with AWS WAFv2 RateBasedRule; compliance-gateway exposes compliance_gateway_throttle_total metric and server.mjs handles throttled actions.
controls.raw_output: infra/terraform/modules/waf/main.tf: variable rate_limit { default = 2000 }; tools/compliance-gateway/src/metrics.mjs: declareMetric('compliance_gateway_throttle_total'); tools/compliance-gateway/src/server.mjs: action: 'query:throttled'

controls.name: Published security contact
controls.evidence: SECURITY.md in repo root with security@gtcx.io, acknowledgement SLA 2 business days, and scope. Bug bounty policy published in docs/security/bug-bounty-policy.md. security.txt not deployed.
controls.raw_output: SECURITY.md: Email: security@gtcx.io; Response SLA: Acknowledgement within 2 business days; docs/security/bug-bounty-policy.md: GTCX Bug Bounty Program Policy

controls.name: Incident notification SLA
controls.evidence: docs/security/security-policy.md documents Medium severity acknowledgement within 72 hours. docs/security/bug-bounty-activation-checklist.md documents severity assignment ≤72 hours. docs/security/security-architecture.md documents incident response time <1h. No standalone partner-specific notification SLA doc.
controls.raw_output: docs/security/security-policy.md: Acknowledge within 72 hours; docs/security/bug-bounty-activation-checklist.md: Severity assignment: ≤72 hours; docs/security/security-architecture.md: Incident response time < 1h

controls.name: No PII in partner-facing systems
controls.evidence: Zero PII field references (ssn, passport, id_number, dob, birth) in tools/compliance-gateway/src/. Architecture docs (ADR-018) confirm no production PII in test data. Tenant codes in observability are explicitly noted as non-PII.
controls.raw_output: grep -ri ssn/passport/id_number/dob/birth tools/compliance-gateway/src/: no results; ADR-018: Anonymized seed data only — no production PII

controls.name: Annual self-assessment questionnaire
controls.evidence: No dedicated partner self-assessment questionnaire. Vendor risk program (docs/compliance/vendor-risk-program.md) contains standardized questionnaires and self-attestation templates for third parties, which can be adapted for partner assessments.
controls.raw_output: docs/compliance/vendor-risk-program.md: Standard questionnaire, Self-attestation templates for vendor tiers

recommendations: Deploy .well-known/security.txt on gtcx.io to improve security contact discoverability.
Create a dedicated partner-facing incident notification SLA document.
Publish a partner-specific security self-assessment questionnaire template.
